 {"id":"bd-c362","title":"Extract database search logic into helper function","description":"The logic for finding a database in a beads directory is duplicated:
- FindDatabasePath() BEADS_DIR section (beads.go:141-169)
- findDatabaseInTree() (beads.go:248-280)

Both implement the same search order:
1. Check config.json first (single source of truth)
2. Fall back to canonical beads.db
3. Search for *.db files, filtering backups and vc.db

Refactoring suggestion:
Extract to a helper function like:
  func findDatabaseInBeadsDir(beadsDir string) string

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for database search logic
- Easier to maintain and update search order
- Reduces code duplication

Related to [deleted:bd-e16b] implementation.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-02T18:34:02.831543-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:17:13.73482-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T22:27:33.794656-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-c4rq","title":"Refactor: Move staleness check inside daemon branch","description":"## Problem

Currently ensureDatabaseFresh() is called before the daemon mode check, but it checks daemonClient != nil internally and returns early. This is redundant.

**Location:** All read commands (list.go:196, show.go:27, ready.go:102, status.go:80, etc.)

## Current Pattern

Call happens before daemon check, function checks daemonClient internally.

## Better Pattern

Move staleness check to direct mode branch only, after daemon check.

## Impact
Low - minor performance improvement (avoids one function call per command in daemon mode)

## Effort
Medium - requires refactoring 8 command files

## Priority
Low - can defer to future cleanup PR","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-11-20T20:17:45.119583-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-20T20:17:45.119583-05:00"}
 {"id":"bd-d4i","title":"Create tip system infrastructure for contextual hints","description":"Implement a tip/hint system that shows helpful contextual messages after successful commands. This is different from the existing error-path "Hint:" messages - tips appear on success paths to educate users about features they might not know about.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:15.693956-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:17:13.735266-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:47:30.747566-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-e166","title":"Improve timestamp comparison readability in import","description":"The timestamp comparison logic uses double-negative which can be confusing:

Current code:
if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // skip update
}

More readable:
if incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // perform update
} else {
    // skip (local is newer)
}

This is a minor refactor for code clarity.

Related: bd-1022

 {"id":"bd-f0n","title":"Git history fallback missing timeout - could hang on large repos","description":"## Problem

The git commands in `checkGitHistoryForDeletions` have no timeout. On large repos with extensive history, `git log --all -S` or `git log --all -G` can take a very long time (minutes).

## Location
`internal/importer/importer.go:899` and `:930`

## Impact
- Import could hang indefinitely
- User has no feedback that git search is running
- No way to cancel except killing the process

## Fix
Add context with timeout to git commands:

```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
defer cancel()
cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", ...)
```

Also consider adding a `--since` flag to bound the git history search.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-25T12:48:24.388639-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:53.669714-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:04:53.669714-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ge7","title":"Improve Beads test coverage from 46% to 80%","description":"","design":"Currently at 46% test coverage. Need to systematically improve coverage across all subsystems, focusing first on packages with minimal or no tests.

Target: 80% overall coverage

Approach:
- Break down by subsystem (internal/* packages)
- Prioritize packages with 0-1 test files
- Each child issue targets specific coverage goals
- Focus on unit tests for core logic, error paths, and edge cases

This epic will be executed by the VC executor to test its ability to handle sustained multi-issue work.","acceptance_criteria":"- Overall test coverage reaches 80% or higher
- All internal/* packages have at least 60% coverage
- All packages with only 1 test file now have at least 3 test files
- Quality gates (go test, golangci-lint) pass
- Tests are maintainable and test actual behavior, not implementation details","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-20T21:21:03.700271-05:00","updated_at":"2025-11-20T21:21:03.700271-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-m7ge","title":"Add .beads/README.md during 'bd init' for project documentation and promotion","description":"When 'bd init' is run, automatically generate a .beads/README.md file that:

1. Briefly explains what Beads is (AI-native issue tracking that lives in your repo)
2. Links to the main repository: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads
3. Provides a quick reference of essential commands:
   - bd create: Create new issues
   - bd list: View all issues
   - bd update: Modify issue status/details
   - bd show: View issue details
   - bd sync: Sync with git remote
4. Highlights key benefits for AI coding agents and developers
5. Encourages developers to try it out

The README should be enthusiastic and compelling to get open source contributors excited about using Beads for their AI-assisted development workflows.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-16T22:32:50.478681-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:17:13.73574-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:49:42.558381-08:00"}

Need workaround or optimization for context size.

 {"id":"bd-pbj","title":"bd doctor --fix should run bd sync to commit untracked deletions.jsonl","description":"After running bd cleanup -f, the deletions.jsonl file is created but left untracked. bd doctor --fix should notice this and run bd sync to commit it.

Current behavior:
- bd cleanup -f creates/updates deletions.jsonl
- git status shows deletions.jsonl as untracked
- bd doctor --fix does not notice or fix this

Expected behavior:
- bd doctor --fix should detect untracked .beads/*.jsonl files
- Should offer to run bd sync to commit them

Related: The git hooks were also missing deletions.jsonl from their staging loops - that's been fixed in this session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T20:29:33.901199-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T20:33:03.296592-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T20:33:03.296592-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-qsm","title":"Auto-compact deletions during bd sync","description":"Parent: bd-imj

## Task
Optionally prune deletions manifest during sync when threshold exceeded.

**Note: Opt-in feature** - disabled by default to avoid sync latency.

## Implementation

In `bd sync`:
```go
func (s *Syncer) Sync() error {
    // ... existing sync logic ...
    
    // Auto-compact only if enabled
    if s.config.GetBool("deletions.auto_compact", false) {
        deletionCount := deletions.Count(".beads/deletions.jsonl")
        threshold := s.config.GetInt("deletions.auto_compact_threshold", 1000)
        
        if deletionCount > threshold {
            retentionDays := s.config.GetInt("deletions.retention_days", 7)
            if err := s.compactor.PruneDeletions(retentionDays); err != nil {
                log.Warnf("Failed to auto-compact deletions: %v", err)
                // Non-fatal, continue sync
            }
        }
    }
    
    // ... rest of sync ...
}
```

## Configuration
```yaml
deletions:
  retention_days: 7
  auto_compact: false              # Opt-in, disabled by default
  auto_compact_threshold: 1000     # Trigger when > N entries (if enabled)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Auto-compact disabled by default
- [ ] Enabled via config `deletions.auto_compact: true`
- [ ] Sync checks deletion count only when enabled
- [ ] Auto-prunes when threshold exceeded
- [ ] Failure is non-fatal (logged warning)
- [ ] Test: no compaction when disabled
- [ ] Test: compaction triggers when enabled and threshold exceeded","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-25T09:57:04.522795-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T15:03:01.469629-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T15:03:01.469629-08:00"}
